cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down this question about causation. We are told: - Karen had a rare hereditary disease called Karpinson's hemotrysoma. - This disease causes an allergic reaction to fermented drinks like wine and liqueurs. - Karen was out to dinner with her boss Mr. Carlson who ordered her meal. - Mr. Carlson chose Moules Mariniere (which had wine in the sauce) instead of Scallops Meuniere (which had no wine). - Karen had the Moules Mariniere, got ill, had convulsions, and died. - The question is whether Mr. Carlson's choice of dish caused Karen's death. Let's analyze causation from the perspective of a typical person: For most people, causation involves two elements:
